Chapter: Spending Authority Policy.
Current document status: Draft.

Section: Spending Authority Policy.

Section: Purpose.

This policy defines who may approve spending and when additional review, committee approval, or church vote may be required.

Dollar amounts are placeholders until reviewed by the Finance Committee.

Note: Congregation Final Authority.
The congregation should approve the annual budget and major non-budgeted expenses where KBC bylaws, budget, policy, amount, or church practice require a vote.

Section: Spending Authority Matrix.

Table summary.
Spending Type: Routine ministry spending within approved budget. Suggested Threshold: Up to $TBD per purchase. Approval Needed: Ministry leader or assigned budget owner. Notes: Receipt required; reviewed monthly..
Spending Type: Routine operating expense within approved budget. Suggested Threshold: Up to $TBD per purchase. Approval Needed: Treasurer, Bookkeeper if delegated, or assigned approver. Notes: Examples: utilities, supplies, subscriptions already approved..
Spending Type: Outside-budget expense. Suggested Threshold: Any amount. Approval Needed: Finance Committee review before purchase. Notes: May require Deacon or church review depending on amount and purpose..
Spending Type: Over-budget expense. Suggested Threshold: Any amount that exceeds approved budget line. Approval Needed: Finance Committee review before purchase. Notes: Determine whether budget transfer or church approval is required..
Spending Type: Large purchase. Suggested Threshold: Over $TBD. Approval Needed: Finance Committee recommendation. Notes: May require Deacon review or church vote..
Spending Type: Capital item / equipment. Suggested Threshold: Over $TBD. Approval Needed: Finance Committee recommendation. Notes: Include funding source, maintenance, and stewardship impact..
Spending Type: Recurring commitment or contract. Suggested Threshold: Any recurring obligation over $TBD per month or $TBD per year. Approval Needed: Finance Committee review. Notes: May require church vote if it affects budget or long-term obligation..
Spending Type: Major non-budgeted project. Suggested Threshold: Over $TBD or otherwise significant to church stewardship. Approval Needed: Finance Committee recommendation, with Deacon counsel if church practice expects it. Notes: Should come to the congregation when bylaws, budget practice, amount, or church expectation require a vote..
Spending Type: Emergency purchase. Suggested Threshold: Up to $TBD if delay would create harm or prevent urgent need. Approval Needed: Pastor, Treasurer, Deacon chair, or Finance chair, with after-the-fact review. Notes: Document reason and report to Finance Committee..
Spending Type: Church vote item. Suggested Threshold: TBD by bylaws, budget, or church policy. Approval Needed: Congregational approval. Notes: Examples may include budget adoption, major purchases, debt, staff compensation, or role creation..

Section: General Rules.

All spending must have a church purpose.
All spending must have documentation.
The person benefiting from a payment should not be the only approver.
Budgeted does not mean undocumented.
Unbudgeted or over-budget spending should be reviewed before purchase whenever practical.
Exceptions should be reported to the Finance Committee.
No one person should have too much control over church money.
Deacon counsel may be helpful for major or sensitive matters, but it should not replace Finance Committee review or congregational approval where required.

Section: Review And Revision.

The Finance Committee should review this policy at least annually or when budget practice changes.

Needs Finance Review: Dollar thresholds, approval roles, emergency authority, contract authority, and review cadence must be confirmed by Finance Committee before adoption.

Needs Bylaw Review: Church vote triggers and final approval path must be checked against KBC Constitution and Bylaws, budget requirements, and church practice.

End of chapter: Spending Authority Policy.
